nodes.title: call
nodes.body:
## Choose France — CNRS AI Rising Talents (5th edition)

Source: https://www.ins2i.cnrs.fr/fr/cnrsinfo/appel-choose-france-cnrs-ai-rising-talents

**Deadline: April 6, 2026** → AI.ChooseFrance@cnrs.fr
Shortlisting end of April · Interviews June · Recruitment H2 2026

---

### EN

As part of the French Strategy in Artificial Intelligence (AI), the Choose France - CNRS AI Rising Talents program offers exceptionally talented early-career AI researchers with the opportunity to establish and lead a research program and team for a duration of 4 years, enhancing their potential to become leading scientists. This program is open to current postdocs as well as junior professors or researchers, with the primary criterion having an outstanding research track record. Applications are welcome from those who earned their PhD either in France or abroad, as soon as they demonstrate substantial international research experience, including mentoring students and managing scientific projects.

This fifth edition of the Choose France program is open to filling two positions. The candidates must demonstrate outstanding track records in machine learning (ML) and related fields, such as computer vision, natural language processing, robotics, etc. Candidates working at the interface of AI and other sciences – neurosciences, biology, physics, chemistry, humanities and social sciences – who have shown how ML approaches have helped to conduct cutting-edge research in their domains are also encouraged to apply. To attract the best talents, the program will provide customizable integration options tailored to candidates' varying degrees of seniority and experience, including fixed-term or permanent contracts.

The program offers competitive salaries as well as an environment for research in the form of a package going from 750k€ to 1 M€, depending on experience, for the period (including gross employer costs for the awarded candidate and other expenses for hiring students and postdocs).

The selected candidates will be recruited by CNRS in one of its research units in France. Candidates must propose at least two laboratories to host their research project. They must contact the directors of these laboratories and explain in their research proposal how their project aligns with the chosen laboratories. The final appointment decision will be made by the CNRS after candidates are selected and interviewed by an international hearing committee. Candidates are expected to contribute to the French AI Strategy, and applications that align with initiatives like ClusterAI institutes or related local projects are encouraged.

The CNRS has been actively engaged in core AI and interdisciplinary research initiatives to understand the implications of AI-driven transformations in scientific practices and social dynamics. To support these efforts, the CNRS has established a rich AI-powered research environment, including the AI for Science and Science for AI (AISSAI) center, which facilitates scientific research at the interface of AI and all other scientific fields. Furthermore, CNRS is also developing open-access platforms and tools to democratize access to AI resources and foster collaboration within research communities. Additionally, the CNRS operates the Jean Zay supercomputer, one of Europe's leading AI computing facilities, boasting with a peak power of 126 petaFlops. By continuously investing in cutting-edge hardware and software infrastructures and regularly updating these facilities, the CNRS ensures that the computing and research capabilities in AI remain at the forefront worldwide.

**Application requirements:**
- A Curriculum vitae
- A report describing previous work
- A comprehensive list of publications, as well as a presentation of the most meaningful publications explaining the personal contributions and the full text of some of them (attach up to three publications)
- A report describing the proposed research programme, including a description of the programme's insertion within two or more CNRS laboratories (up to 5 pages excluding bibliography)
- Three references' contacts

**Evaluation:** The applications are evaluated by a selection committee, composed of leading scientists in the domain, who evaluate proposals on the basis of excellence. Candidate that are short-listed will be invited to an interview by videoconference.

---

### FR

Dans le cadre de la Stratégie nationale pour l'intelligence artificielle, le programme *Choose France - CNRS AI Rising Talents* offre l'opportunité à des chercheurs et chercheuses, en début de carrière et particulièrement talentueux, d'établir et de diriger un programme en IA pour une durée de 4 ans. Ce programme vise à renforcer leur potentiel et leur visibilité. Il est ouvert à des chercheurs et chercheuses ayant déjà un parcours de recherche remarquable après une thèse en France ou à l'étranger et justifiant d'une expérience internationale significative, notamment en matière d'encadrement d'étudiants et de portage de projets scientifiques.

Cette cinquième édition du programme *Choose France* est ouverte à deux postes. Les candidats doivent faire preuve d'un parcours exceptionnel dans le domaine de l'apprentissage machine et des domaines connexes, tels que la vision par ordinateur, le traitement du langage naturel, la robotique, etc. Les candidats travaillant à l'interface de l'IA et d'autres sciences - neurosciences, biologie, physique, chimie, sciences humaines et sociales - qui ont montré comment les approches d'apprentissage machine ont aidé à mener des recherches de pointe dans leurs domaines sont également encouragés à postuler. Afin d'attirer les meilleurs talents, le programme proposera des options d'intégration personnalisables adaptées aux différents degrés d'ancienneté et d'expérience des candidats, y compris des contrats à durée déterminée ou indéterminée.

Le programme offre des salaires compétitifs ainsi qu'un environnement de recherche sous la forme d'un package qui, selon l'expérience, est compris entre 750k€ et 1 M€ pour la période (incluant les charges patronales brutes pour le candidat choisi et d'autres dépenses pour l'embauche d'étudiants et de post-doctorants).

Les candidats sélectionnés seront recrutés par le CNRS dans l'une de ses unités en France. Ils doivent proposer au moins deux laboratoires pour accueillir leur projet de recherche en prenant contact avec les directeurs de ces laboratoires et expliquer comment leur projet s'inscrit dans ces laboratoires d'accueil. La décision finale de nomination sera prise par le CNRS après la phase de sélection par un comité d'audition international. Ce choix se portera sur le potentiel des candidats choisis à contribuer à la stratégie française en matière d'IA. Dans ce sens, les candidatures qui s'inscrivent dans le cadre d'initiatives telles que les instituts ClusterAI ou des projets locaux connexes sont encouragées.

Le CNRS s'est engagé activement dans des initiatives de recherche interdisciplinaire et sur l'IA afin de comprendre les implications des transformations induites par l'IA dans les pratiques scientifiques et les dynamiques sociales. Pour soutenir ces efforts, le CNRS a mis en place un environnement de recherche riche en IA, y compris le centre *AI for Science and Science for AI* (AISSAI), qui facilite la recherche scientifique à l'interface de l'IA et de tous les autres domaines scientifiques. En outre, le CNRS développe également des plateformes et des outils en libre accès pour démocratiser l'accès aux ressources de l'IA et favoriser la collaboration au sein des communautés de recherche. Le centre exploite le supercalculateur Jean Zay, l'une des principales installations de calcul intensif en Europe, avec une puissance de pointe de 126 pétaFlops. En investissant continuellement dans des infrastructures matérielles et logicielles de pointe et en mettant régulièrement à jour ces installations, le CNRS veille à ce que les capacités de calcul et de recherche en IA restent à la pointe au niveau mondial.

**Composition du dossier :**
- Un curriculum-vitae
- Un rapport de recherche décrivant les travaux antérieurs
- Une liste exhaustive des publications, ainsi qu'une présentation des publications les plus significatives expliquant les contributions personnelles et le texte intégral de certaines d'entre elles (joindre jusqu'à trois publications)
- Un rapport décrivant le programme de recherche proposé ; y compris une description de l'insertion du programme dans deux ou plusieurs laboratoires du CNRS (jusqu'à 5 pages hors bibliographie)
- Trois contacts de référents

**Évaluation :** Les candidatures sont évaluées par un comité de sélection, composé de scientifiques de premier plan dans le domaine, qui évaluent les propositions sur la base de l'excellence scientifique. Si le candidat est présélectionné, il sera invité à un entretien par vidéoconférence.

nodes.title: pitch
nodes.body:
I build AI systems that do science, and I prove it in cosmology. The frameworks emerge from doing it.

**Priority ordering:**
1. **Agentic science** — build frameworks that accelerate science by *doing* science. Meet the pace of the exponential. Quality maintained through infrastructure, not gatekeeping.
2. **Verification / comprehensibility / democracy** — emerges from #1. When agents produce faster than humans read, you need tools for that. European AI sovereignty is not abstract (Anthropic/DoD, Feb 2026).
3. **Alignment** — informed by practice, not theoretical.

**NOT pitching as:** ML method developer, philosopher of science, or meta-researcher on AI workflows. Pitching as someone who will produce science with AI agents and, in doing so, build the infrastructure everyone needs.

**Sub-fibers:**
- agentic science frameworks — the core: agents doing end-to-end science
- tapestry as science DAG — inspectable computation record
- felt as workflow infrastructure — agent coordination across sessions
- AI×physics framing — how to position for the committee
- cosmology as proving ground — where the work happens

nodes.title: labs & people
nodes.body:
Must propose ≥2 CNRS labs as hosts. Contact lab directors. Explain alignment in the 5pp.

ClusterAI alignment is "encouraged" but not required — it's a narrative bonus. Choose host labs for actual fit; frame cluster alignment in the text.

**Host labs (decided 2026-03-14):**
- **IAS** (Institut d'Astrophysique Spatiale, Orsay) — CNRS/Université Paris-Saclay. Giulio Fabbian (supportive, WhatsApp 2026-03-12). Group leaders: Laura & Adélie. Director: Frédéric Auchère.
- **CosmoStat** (CEA Saclay) — everyone already on board re AI future

**Previous candidate (dropped):**
- ~~IAP~~ (Institut d'Astrophysique de Paris) — Kumiko Kotera contacted 2026-03-07, Galli/McCracken supportive. Replaced by IAS for stronger personal connection (Giulio) and Saclay ecosystem coherence.

**ClusterAI:** see sub-fiber. DATAIA (Saclay) is the strongest narrative fit — both IAS and CosmoStat are in the Saclay ecosystem.

**Applications aligning with ClusterAI institutes or related local projects are encouraged** — but the cluster connection lives in the 5pp text, not the org chart.

**⚠ INS2I question (2026-03-16).** Sabouret raised concern that the call may prefer or require host labs affiliated with INS2I (CNRS informatics unit). Neither CosmoStat (CEA) nor IAS are INS2I labs. Two inquiries in flight: Nicolas asking a contact, and we're emailing Choose France. If confirmed, need to identify INS2I labs with cosmology/AI-for-science fit. See `ins2i-lab-eligibility-b6a8d8c0`.
nodes.outcome: Two host labs: CosmoStat/DAp (CEA Paris-Saclay, UMR AIM) and IAP (Sorbonne, UMR 7095). Contacts made: Stéphane Mathis (DAp director, emailed 2026-03-07), Kumiko Kotera (IAP director, emailed 2026-03-07), Nicolas Sabouret (DATAIA, emailed 2026-03-10 via Michelle Lochner). ClusterAI alignment encouraged not required — hard requirement is ≥2 CNRS labs. Named collaborators: Starck, Kilbinger, Farrens (CosmoStat); Galli, Hivon, McCracken (IAP).

nodes.title: requirements
nodes.body:
Five dossier components — see sub-fibers for each with exact call language.

1. **CV** — must demonstrate: outstanding track record, international experience, student supervision, project management
2. **Research report** — report describing previous work, no page limit specified
3. **Publications** — comprehensive list + narrative presentation of up to 3 highlighted papers with full texts
4. **Research program 5pp** — the pitch materialized, including ≥2 CNRS lab integration (links to pitch + labs & people)
5. **Referees** — 3 contacts (not letters)

**Key asset:** upcoming cosmology paper written ~90% by LLM agents — existence proof that frames the whole track record.
nodes.outcome: Dossier compiled March 2026. CV (9pp): full career, 37 publications via ADS-verified bibliography, agentic science + international collaborations highlighted. Research report (3pp): CMB lensing, UNIONS cosmic shear, kinematic lensing, AU Mic. Publications narrative (5pp): 37 journal/preprint publications, 3 highlighted (Daley 2019 AU Mic, Dutcher 2021 SPT-3G, Daley 2026 UNIONS). All documents use EB Garamond/Raleway typography, compile cleanly.

nodes.title: referees
nodes.body:
**From the call:** "Three references' contacts" / "Trois contacts de référents"

Not letters — just contact info for three people the committee can reach out to.

**Prospective:** Martin, Sam, François, Silvia, Henry, Gil, Marius?

- diversity of french labs vs PhD advisors etc. who don't know about my AI usage?

## Comments
**2026-03-14 19:39** — Giulio Fabbian (IAS) is now a strong referee candidate — supportive of application, direct collaborator context.
nodes.outcome: Three referee contacts required — 'trois contacts de référents.' These are contacts the committee may reach out to, NOT recommendation letters. No letters needed.

nodes.title: research program 5pp
nodes.body:
**From the call:** "A report describing the proposed research programme, including a description of the programme's insertion within two or more CNRS laboratories (up to 5 pages excluding bibliography)"

5 pages max (excl. bibliography). Must include: the research program itself AND how it integrates into ≥2 CNRS labs. Candidates must contact lab directors and explain alignment. Applications aligning with ClusterAI institutes or related local projects are encouraged. Candidates are expected to contribute to the French AI Strategy.

## Comments
**2026-03-05 17:19** — First full draft completed in cnrs-5pp/ Overleaf repo. 4 pages (limit 5 excl. bibliography). 5 sections: bitter lesson, agentic science in practice, research program, verification, lab integration. LaTeX with natbib, references.bib has 9 ADS-verified entries.

nodes.title: ClusterAI
nodes.body:
9 IA Clusters funded under France 2030, €360M total. The call "encourages" alignment with ClusterAI institutes — it's a narrative bonus, not a requirement. The hard requirement is ≥2 CNRS host labs.

**Strategy:** choose host labs for actual fit, then frame narrative alignment with whichever cluster(s) are nearby in the 5pp.

Four clusters researched for alignment (by fit):

| Cluster | € | Where | Agentic science hook | Contact |
|---------|---|-------|---------------------|---------|
| **PostGenAI@PARIS** | 35M | Sorbonne | Core concept = agentic AI. AI4Science launching Mar 2026. Physics-aware DL chair. | Stasenko (indirect), Gallinari, Biau |
| **DATAIA** | 20M | Paris-Saclay | "E-science" named domain. Known Equations pillar. DeMythif.AI. | Sabouret (light), Pascal |
| **PR[AI]RIE–PSAI** | 75M | PSL | Statistical physics names cosmology. AI4theSciences doctoral program. | None yet |
| **Hi! PARIS** | 70M | IP Paris | "AI for Sciences" named but underpopulated — white space. | None yet |

Not researched: ANITI (Toulouse), 3IA Côte d'Azur (Nice), ENACT (Lorraine), SequoIA (Rennes), MIAI (Grenoble).
nodes.outcome: 9 IA Clusters funded under France 2030, €360M total. Call encourages alignment — narrative bonus, not requirement. Four relevant institutes: PostGenAI@PARIS (launching 2026, includes AI4Science with Lavaux at IAP doing AI4Astrophysics), PR[AI]RIE-PSAI (IAP not a member — but DAp/CosmoStat is in Paris-Saclay ecosystem), DATAIA (Paris-Saclay, Sabouret contacted), Hi! PARIS (HEC/IP Paris). Section 4 of 5pp frames these as natural homes for the verification/diffusion agenda.

nodes.title: Knuth: Claude's Cycles (March 2026)
nodes.outcome: Donald Knuth wrote a 5-page paper describing how Claude Opus 4.6 solved an open combinatorial problem (Hamiltonian cycle decomposition) he'd been working on for weeks, in about an hour. Written Feb 28 / revised Mar 4, 2026. Postscript: GPT-5.3-codex solved the even case too. Cited in proposal Section 1.

nodes.title: CNRS Rising Talents: full proposal
nodes.body:
A complete, compelling CNRS AI Rising Talents application that wins one of two positions. Deadline April 6, 2026.

## Desired State

Five documents, ready to submit, each reinforcing the others:

1. **Research program (5pp)** — `cnrs-5pp/main.tex`. Uses all 5 pages (excluding bibliography). Every sentence earns its space. The argument: I do cosmology with agentic AI, and in doing so develop the practice for all of science. The document should feel like the most natural thing a committee of leading AI+science researchers could fund — inevitable, not speculative. ClusterAI alignment is woven into the narrative (PostGenAI, DATAIA), not bolted on. The lab integration section names specific people and explains *why* those labs, not just *that* those labs.

2. **CV** — `cnrs-5pp/cv.tex` (or standalone). Demonstrates: outstanding track record, international experience (Canada → France, 3 international collaborations), student supervision (multiple master's students), project management (MOU lead for SPT×Euclid). Concise, well-formatted.

3. **Research report** — `cnrs-5pp/research-report.tex`. Previous work, no page limit but aim for ~3-5 pages. Shows how ML/AI approaches have helped conduct cutting-edge research. The UNIONS paper written ~90% by agents is the crown jewel — but the report should also cover the cosmology itself (weak lensing, CMB lensing, kinematic lensing).

4. **Publications narrative** — `cnrs-5pp/publications.tex`. Comprehensive list + presentation of 3 highlighted papers explaining personal contributions. The 3 chosen papers should demonstrate range (cosmology + AI methodology) and leadership.

5. **Referees** — 3 contacts. Strategy decided, names confirmed. Diversity of perspective: someone who knows the cosmology, someone who knows the AI practice, someone who knows the French landscape.

## Voice

Researcher, not builder. Infrastructure emerges from doing science — the wake, not the destination. Verbs: conducting, developing, investigating, leading, supervising. NOT: I built, I created, I founded. The writing should be direct, confident, precise — a scientist describing what they do and why it matters. Not selling. Not hedging. Not philosophizing. The prose of someone who is doing the work and knows it.

## Context

### Tapestry
The full decision DAG lives in `.felt/` with tag `tapestry:cnrs`. Start each iteration with `felt tree call-requirements-e67445bf` to see the full structure. Key fibers:
- `call-requirements-e67445bf` — exact call text (EN + FR), evaluation criteria
- `research-program-83cc4069` — pitch framing and priority ordering
- `host-labs-7a2b0e1e` — CosmoStat + IAP, lab director contacts needed
- `clusterai-2b0c4530` — four clusters researched, PostGenAI + DATAIA are best fits
- `writing-voice-researcher-not-a18cedb6` — voice rules
- `lightcone-don-t-mention-in-631ac82b` — keep Lightcone Research out of draft

### Key files
- `cnrs-5pp/main.tex` — 5pp draft (5 pages content + 1 bib, 5 sections). Compiles cleanly.
- `cnrs-5pp/research-report.tex` — research report (~3 pages). Compiles cleanly.
- `cnrs-5pp/publications-narrative.tex` — publications narrative (37 papers, 3 highlighted, 5 pages). Compiles cleanly.
- `application/cvitae.tex` — CV (9 pages with publications). Compiles cleanly with unsrtnat.
- `cnrs-5pp/references.bib` — ADS-verified bibliography (9 entries)
- `cifre-pitch.md` — prior pitch material with extensive landscape research
- `manifesto.md` — philosophical framing (don't import the voice, but the ideas are relevant)

### French AI landscape
- **PostGenAI@PARIS** (Sorbonne, €35M) — "post-generative = agentic AI." AI4Science launched March 9, 2026. Research domains: AI4Chemistry (Piquemal), AI4Materials (Saitta), AI4Biology (Carbone), AI4Climate (Monteleoni), **AI4Astrophysics (Guilhem Lavaux, IAP)**, Physics-aware AI (Gallinari). Keynotes: Duraisamy ("agentic AI infrastructures for scientific workflows"), Thaler ("Centaur Science"). Key contacts: Gérard Biau (SCAI), Patrick Gallinari (ISIR).
- **DATAIA** (Paris-Saclay, €20M) — "E-science" named domain. Known Equations pillar. DeMythif.AI doctoral program. Key contact: Frédéric Pascal.
- **AISSAI** — CNRS UAR (since Jan 2024), jointly supervised by INS2I + INSMI. Director: Jalal Fadili (ENSICAEN). Thematic trimesters: AI+Statistical Physics, Causality, AI+Digital Humanities, AI+Particle Physics, "AI for the Two Infinites" (incl. astrophysics). Also: AstroInfo hackathon (2023), AI+Science Summer School (with UChicago Paris Center), international fellows (12-24 months), postdoc program. Mentioned in the call text. See fiber `aissai-research-and-details-aaa70253`.
- **Jean Zay** — CNRS supercomputer, 126 PF, mentioned in call text.
- **French AI Strategy** — €2.5B via France 2030, €360M for 9 IA Clusters, PEPR AI (CNRS+Inria+CEA, €45M for 9 projects), €109B private investment announced Feb 2025. Priorities: sovereignty, competitiveness, responsible AI. Training 40k-100k students/year in AI.

### What the committee wants
From the call: "outstanding track records in machine learning and related fields." But also: "Candidates working at the interface of AI and other sciences who have shown how ML approaches have helped to conduct cutting-edge research in their domains are *also encouraged to apply*." The proposal must make the case that doing cosmology with agentic AI IS cutting-edge AI research — that the practice itself generates methodological insight, not just scientific results.

### Collaborators mentioned in current draft
- François Lanusse (CNRS, CosmoStat) — but Lightcone must stay out
- Silvia Galli (IAP) — SPT connection
- Henry McCracken (IAP) — AI-forward practices
- **Potential:** Guilhem Lavaux (IAP) — presenting AI4Astrophysics at PostGenAI AI4Science. See question fiber `question-add-guilhem-lavaux-iap-385d0156`.

## Skills

Activate before working:
- `/writing` — for prose quality, voice, rhythm
- `/tapestry` — for filing claims and updating the DAG
- `/managing-bibliography` — for any new citations needed (ADS API)

## Evidence

### 5pp quality
- Compiles: `cd cnrs-5pp && pdflatex main && bibtex main && pdflatex main && pdflatex main`
- Page count: the PDF should be 5 pages of content + bibliography (check visually or via `pdftotext main.pdf - | wc -w` — target ~3000-3500 words)
- No orphan sections squeezed at the bottom of a page
- Every claim has evidence or citation
- ClusterAI names (PostGenAI, DATAIA) appear with specific program names, not just as labels
- Lab integration names specific people and their relevance

### Other documents
- Each exists as a `.tex` file in `cnrs-5pp/`
- Each compiles cleanly
- Research report references the UNIONS agent-written paper as concrete evidence
- Publications list is comprehensive (check ADS for Cail Daley publications)
- CV includes: education, positions, publications summary, collaborations, supervision, talks

### Tapestry
- New decisions and discoveries filed as fibers
- `felt ls -t tapestry:cnrs` shows the growing DAG

## Decisions (resolved)

- **Page budget:** The extra page goes to (1) a concrete 4-year research timeline/milestones in Section 3, and (2) deeper verification methodology in Section 4 — concrete examples of what verification practices look like in agentic science.
- **5pp page fill (DONE):** Diffusion expanded to full subsection (3-scale argument), robustness paragraph added, 250x→2,500x math fixed, lab integration tightened. Result: exactly 5 pages content + 1 page bibliography.
- **Other documents:** Full draft attempt. Pull CV from the git repo (Cail will add it). Draft research report and publications narrative from ADS + tapestry context. Cail reviews and corrects.
- **PostGenAI AI4Science:** Not attending the March 9 launch. Frame alignment through published program descriptions and existing institutional connections only — no "I will present" framing.
- **CV source:** Found at `application/cvitae.tex`. Compiles with locally-provided packages (aas_macros, bibunits, currvita, enumitem). Uses unsrtnat bibliography style. Publication .bib files complete (3 lead-author + 32 collaboration papers).
- **AISSAI connection (DONE):** Yes — mentioned in both Section 4 (Diffusion: workshop at AISSAI, Year 3) and Section 5 (Lab integration: extending AISSAI's mission from AI methods to AI agents).
- **CV updated:** Added international collaborations section, master's supervision, MOU leadership, agentic science to research interests and skills, current position description. Removed TOSCA framing. Fixed \aaa macro and Unicode Lambda in bib files.
- **Research report updated:** Fixed kinematic lensing timeline (2024-2025 not 2023), tightened prose, fixed ΛCDM Unicode, made ML claims more accurate for CMB lensing section.
- **Quality audit (DONE):** Cross-document consistency verified. Fixed: AU Mic described as "cosmology" (it's debris disks), CMB lensing kernel z~0.5→z~2, ML overstatements in publications narrative and research report removed. Research report tightened from 4→3 pages: Overview section removed (arrive through content), sales-pitch diffusion bullets replaced with substantive prose. ADS citation counts verified (AU Mic: 61 ✓, Dutcher 2021: 263 ✓). SPT data years clarified: Dutcher 2021 = 2018 data, dissertation/CV = 2019+2020 data (both correct, different datasets).
- **Fact-check (DONE):** Verified all Section 1 claims against primary sources. METR numbers updated to v1.1 (March 3, 2026): 80% threshold ~70h, 50% threshold ~720h, doubling 129d from 176d. Knuth "in roughly an hour" removed (not in source). CMBAgent "placed first ahead of teams" → "won first-place award, outperforming many domain experts." AISSAI "Astro-ML" → "AI for the Two Infinites" (actual program name). Dodelson claim verified accurate.
- **Knuth timing claim (DONE):** Previous fact-check intended to remove "in roughly an hour" but residual "about one hour into a guided session" persisted. Verified against full Knuth paper text: NO timing information anywhere. Removed. The paper describes Claude solving the problem via numbered "explorations" with human coaching (Filip Stappers), no session duration given.
- **Agentic tools overstatement (DONE):** "the most widely used agentic coding tools contain no code older than three months" → "the dominant agentic coding tools have been substantially rewritten multiple times in the past year alone" — defensible with AI-expert committee.
- **PostGenAI AI4Science reference (DONE):** Updated 5pp to mention "AI for astrophysics" as a named research domain within PostGenAI's AI4Science program, replacing vague "launching in 2026."
- **Institutional accuracy (DONE):** CosmoStat is part of UMR AIM (7158, CEA/CNRS/Université Paris Cité/Université Paris-Saclay), not a standalone "joint CEA-CNRS laboratory." IAP is UMR 7095 (CNRS/Sorbonne Université). Both now have accurate institutional designations in the 5pp. This matters: the committee is CNRS researchers who know the UMR system.
- **Euclid deep fields consistency (DONE):** Research targets said "one" while timeline said "two." Targets now say "observable from the South Pole" (agnostic); timeline retains "two" as the specific Year 2 milestone. EDFS (Dec -48°) is confirmed observable (Archipley 2026); EDFF (Dec -28°) is plausible with dedicated observations.
- **Publications narrative Euclid section (DONE):** Empty Euclid subsection removed. Merged into "UNIONS and Euclid" with note pointing to Archipley 2026 under SPT.
- **All previous uncommitted changes now committed (DONE):** cnrs-5pp submodule commit aad032b consolidates all fixes from iterations 8e1915a6, 07ada4ac, 04615244, and 50d1d268. Parent repo commit f8ded35 tracks the submodule.

## Open Questions

- **Which 3 papers to highlight?** Current: (1) Daley 2019 AU Mic, (2) Dutcher 2021 SPT-3G, (3) Daley 2026 UNIONS. The AU Mic paper shows independence but has zero connection to AI/ML — is this the right choice for a committee of AI researchers? Alternatives: Pan 2023 (CMB lensing, pipeline lead), Ge 2025 (delensed EE, uses Cail's maps), Qu 2026 (PRL, joint CMB lensing). Need Cail's input on actual contributions to each.
- **Referees:** Martin Kilbinger? Sam Passaglia? François Lanusse? Someone from the AI side? The committee is AI researchers — a referee who can speak to the AI methodology is valuable.
- **How explicit about the agent-written paper?** Current draft says "produced by AI agents under my supervision." Is this the right level of detail for a committee of AI researchers? They'll find it compelling — but does it need more specifics about what "supervision" looked like?

## Web Research Needed

Each iteration should check whether these have been researched yet (fibers will exist if so):
- ~~PostGenAI AI4Science program details~~ DONE. Full program: 6 research domains incl. AI4Astrophysics (Guilhem Lavaux, IAP). Keynotes on agentic AI and centaur science. Fiber `postgenai-ai4science-launch-c3620987`.
- ~~DATAIA e-science program specifics~~ DONE. Detailed in fiber `dataia-13603ec7`.
- ~~AISSAI center~~ DONE. Director: Jalal Fadili. Programs: thematic trimesters, AI+Science summer school, postdoc program, international fellows. Fiber `aissai-research-and-details-aaa70253`.
- ~~French AI Strategy 2025-2030~~ DONE. €2.5B France 2030, €360M IA Clusters, PEPR AI, €109B private investment.
- ~~Cail Daley's ADS publication record~~ DONE. 44 true ADS entries (37 journal/preprint, 7 abstracts).
- ~~CMBAgent paper details~~ DONE. Cited as `laverick.etal24` in references.bib. Won first-place award at NeurIPS 2025 FAIR Universe challenge.
- ~~METR data verification~~ DONE. Updated to v1.1 (March 3, 2026).
- ~~Any recent agentic science papers or results that strengthen the case~~ DONE. Comprehensive survey in fiber `agentic-science-landscape-2025-c1289b35`. Key: SPOT benchmark (6% precision at error detection), ICLR 2025 agentic science workshop, DeepMind Genesis at 17 DOE labs. Findings validate 5pp framing; no changes needed to the document.
nodes.outcome: Application assembled March 2026. 5pp through 8 ralph iterations: major structural revision (4 sections), fact-checking against primary sources, institutional accuracy audit, prose quality passes. Key corrections: METR numbers (12h/129-day doubling from v1.1), Knuth timing claim removed, AISSAI program name fixed, CosmoStat institutional description corrected. Agentic science landscape surveyed — term established (ICLR 2025), cosmology underrepresented (our opening), end-to-end systems fragile (validates practice argument). AISSAI researched — UAR since 2024, AI for the Two Infinites program relevant. Unified dossier document created (dossier.tex) assembling all components.

nodes.title: AISSAI: research and details
nodes.outcome: AISSAI (AI for Science and Science for AI) is a CNRS UAR (since Jan 2024), jointly supervised by INS2I and INSMI. Director: Jalal Fadili (ENSICAEN/GREYC). Programs: thematic trimesters (3-4 months each) including AI+Statistical Physics, Causality, AI+Digital Humanities, AI+Particle Physics, 'AI for the Two Infinites' (particle physics + astrophysics). Also: AstroInfo hackathon (2023, Fréjus), AI+Science Summer School (4th ed. 2025 at UChicago Paris Center), international fellows (12-24 months in CNRS labs), postdoc program (2024 call, Nov 2025 kickoff). Collaborates with IVADO (Quebec). NOTE: No program named 'Astro-ML' exists — previous draft was incorrect. Fixed to reference 'AI for the Two Infinites.' The 5pp says 'AISSAI has already hosted thematic programs relevant to astrophysics' — this is accurate via the Two Infinites program and AstroInfo hackathon.

nodes.title: Agentic science landscape 2025-2026: survey of key papers, systems, and results
nodes.body:
Survey of the agentic science landscape as of March 2026, for context in CNRS AI Rising Talents application. Findings validate the 5pp framing without requiring changes to the document.

## End-to-End Scientific Workflows

- **AI Scientist-v2** (Sakana AI, Apr 2025, arXiv:2504.08066): First AI-generated paper accepted at ICLR 2025 workshop. Agentic tree search for experiment design. Cost ~$6-15/paper. But: independent review (arXiv:2502.14297) found 42% experiment failure rate, poor novelty assessment.
- **AI-Researcher** (HKUDS, May 2025, NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight): Fully autonomous literature→hypothesis→algorithm→manuscript pipeline.
- **Agent Laboratory** (Schmidgall et al., Jan 2025, EMNLP 2025 Findings): End-to-end with specialized agents (PhD, Postdoc, ML Engineer, Professor). 84% cost reduction. Human feedback significantly improves quality.
- **AgentRxiv** (Mar 2025, arXiv:2503.18102): Preprint server for agent-generated research. Multiple autonomous labs sharing findings improved MATH-500 accuracy from 70.2% to 78.2%.
- **OpenAI + Red Queen Bio** (Dec 2025): GPT-5 proposed and iterated on a real molecular cloning protocol, achieving 79x efficiency gain. First wet-lab demonstration.

## Major Lab Deployments

- **DeepMind AI Co-Scientist** (Feb 2025, arXiv:2502.18864): Multi-agent system on Gemini 2.0. Validated drug repurposing candidates in vitro. Deployed to all 17 DOE labs via Genesis Mission.
- **AlphaEvolve** (DeepMind, Jun 2025, arXiv:2506.13131): First improvement to 4×4 complex matrix multiplication in 56 years. 0.7% compute recovery across Google data centers.
- **Gemini Deep Think** (Aug 2025–Feb 2026): Gold-medal IMO, resolved 18 research problems, detected logical flaw in peer-reviewed paper.
- **DOE Genesis Mission** (Dec 2025): National initiative deploying AI across 17 DOE labs. 24 company collaborations.
- **Stanford Biomni** (May 2025): Claude-powered agent for 25 biomedical domains. Claude Opus best-performing model.
- **Allen AI AutoDiscovery** (Feb 2026): Autonomous hypothesis generation from structured datasets. Every result fully auditable.

## Verification Research

- **SPOT Benchmark** (Son et al., May 2025, arXiv:2505.11855): 83 published papers with 91 confirmed errors. Best model (o3): 21.1% recall, 6.1% precision. All other models near zero. Strongest evidence automated verification is not ready.
- **Cornelio et al.** (Sep 2025, arXiv:2509.01398): Position paper arguing verification is THE bottleneck of AI-driven science. "Hypotheses generated faster than they can be verified."

## Community

- **ICLR 2025 Workshop: "Agentic AI for Science"** (Apr 27, 2025, Singapore): First major venue-level workshop on agentic science.
- **FrontierScience Benchmark** (OpenAI, Dec 2025): PhD-level science reasoning. GPT-5.2: 77% Olympiad / 25% Research. 52-point gap reveals models struggle with real scientific work.

## Survey Papers

- Gridach et al. (Mar 2025, arXiv:2503.08979): "Agentic AI for Scientific Discovery: A Survey"
- arXiv:2503.24047 (Mar 2025): "Towards Scientific Intelligence: LLM-based Scientific Agents"
- arXiv:2508.14111 (Aug 2025): "From AI for Science to Agentic Science" — frames the progression as a paradigm shift

## Implications for CNRS Application

1. The term "agentic science" is established. We are not coining — we are positioning.
2. The verification gap is quantified (SPOT). Our Section 4 argument is strongly supported.
3. Cosmology is underrepresented in agentic science demos. This is our opening.
4. End-to-end systems exist but are fragile. The *practice* of doing it well is the missing piece — exactly what the 5pp claims to offer.
5. No changes needed to the 5pp. The document already makes the right argument; these findings validate it.
nodes.outcome: Comprehensive survey completed. Key findings: (1) "Agentic science" is now an established term (ICLR 2025 workshop, 3 survey papers, DOE Genesis Mission). (2) SPOT benchmark shows frontier models achieve only 6% precision / 21% recall at detecting errors in published papers — the verification gap is quantified and real. (3) Most agentic science demos are in biology/chemistry/materials; cosmology is underrepresented — this is our opening. (4) Major labs deploying: DeepMind AI co-scientist at 17 DOE labs, Allen AI AutoDiscovery in production, Stanford Biomni live. (5) End-to-end systems exist but are fragile — AI Scientist-v2 has 42% experiment failure rate. The practice (judgment, verification, methodology) is the missing piece — validates the 5pp argument. No changes needed to the 5pp; findings support the existing framing.

nodes.title: email Frédéric Auchère
nodes.body:
IAS director. Required by call: must contact lab directors. Introduce self, explain agentic AI × cosmology program, ask for brief conversation before April 6.

**Sequencing:** send after Laura Salvati & Adélie Gorce respond (so you can reference their support).

---

## Draft: Email to Frédéric Auchère

To: frederic.auchere@ias.u-psud.fr (verify address)
Subject: CNRS AI Rising Talents — interest in proposing IAS as host lab

Dear Prof. Auchère,

I'm Cail Daley, a postdoc at CEA Saclay working in observational cosmology (CMB lensing, weak lensing, large-scale structure). I'm writing because I'd like to apply for the Choose France CNRS AI Rising Talents program, and I would be interested in proposing IAS as one of my two host laboratories.

My research program is at the intersection of agentic AI and survey cosmology — using AI agents to run end-to-end scientific workflows and developing verification infrastructure for AI-mediated science. I've been in contact with Giulio Fabbian, Laura Salvati, and Adélie Gorce in the cosmology group, who have expressed their support. My second host lab would be CosmoStat at CEA Saclay, where I currently work.

The program requires candidates to contact the directors of their proposed host laboratories. Would you have time for a brief conversation before the April 6 deadline? I'd welcome the chance to explain the project and hear whether it could be a good fit for IAS.

Thank you and all my best,
Cail Daley

nodes.title: email Laura & Adélie (IAS)
nodes.body:
IAS cosmology group leaders. Giulio suggested reaching out as first contact. Introduce self, explain program, explore collaboration angles. Do this Monday 2026-03-16. Then contact Auchère (director) once buy-in established.

**Laura Salvati** — CMB cosmology (overlap with Cail's CMB lensing)
**Adélie Gorce** — reionization, 21cm, EoR

---

## Draft: Email to Laura Salvati

To: laura.salvati@ias.u-psud.fr (verify address)
Subject: CNRS AI Rising Talents — interest in proposing IAS as host lab

Dear Laura,

I'm Cail Daley, a postdoc at CEA Saclay working in observational cosmology — CMB lensing with SPT and Planck, weak lensing, large-scale structure. Giulio Fabbian suggested I reach out to you.

I'm applying for the Choose France CNRS AI Rising Talents program (deadline April 6), which funds 4-year research positions at the intersection of AI and an application domain. My proposal is on agentic AI for survey cosmology — using AI agents to run end-to-end scientific workflows (pipeline construction, systematic cross-checks, multi-probe analysis) and developing the verification infrastructure that makes this trustworthy.

The program asks candidates to propose at least two CNRS host labs. I'd like to propose IAS alongside CosmoStat at CEA Saclay. The cosmology group at IAS — and your work on CMB in particular — would be a natural environment for this kind of research, and the proximity to Saclay makes genuine cross-lab collaboration practical.

Would you be open to a brief conversation about whether this could be a good fit? I'm happy to share the draft research program. I'll also be at Moriond next week if that's easier.

All my best,
Cail Daley

---

## Draft: Email to Adélie Gorce

To: adelie.gorce@ias.u-psud.fr (verify address)
Subject: CNRS AI Rising Talents — interest in proposing IAS as host lab

Dear Adélie,

I'm Cail Daley, a postdoc at CEA Saclay working in observational cosmology — CMB lensing with SPT and Planck, weak lensing, large-scale structure. Giulio Fabbian suggested I reach out to you.

I'm applying for the Choose France CNRS AI Rising Talents program (deadline April 6), which funds 4-year research positions at the intersection of AI and an application domain. My proposal is on agentic AI for survey cosmology — using AI agents to run end-to-end scientific workflows (pipeline construction, systematic cross-checks, multi-probe analysis) and developing the verification infrastructure that makes this trustworthy.

The program asks candidates to propose at least two CNRS host labs. I'd like to propose IAS alongside CosmoStat at CEA Saclay. Giulio mentioned you and Laura Salvati as the cosmology group leaders, and I wanted to introduce myself and the project before reaching out to the lab director.

Would you be open to a brief conversation? I'm happy to share the draft research program. I'll also be at Moriond next week.

All my best,
Cail Daley

nodes.title: Sabouret lunch debrief
nodes.body:
Lunch at Pizzeria F0, Gif-sur-Yvette, 2026-03-16.

## What landed

Sabouret found the proposal idea compelling. Liked the framing of developing practices and sharing them — positioning diffusion as a service to the French research community (increasing AI literacy and frontier science capability). Strongly endorsed proposing benchmarks as a concrete deliverable.

## Risks raised

**INS2I lab preference.** Sabouret thinks the call may be intended for labs associated with INS2I (the CNRS informatics unit). CosmoStat (CEA) and possibly IAS may not qualify. This is the biggest structural risk — it could mean the host lab choice needs to change entirely. Nicolas is checking with a contact; we're also emailing Choose France directly.

**Weak research track record.** Two dimensions:
1. Short publication list — mitigated by the nature of observational cosmology: collaborations of hundreds, 5+ year timelines for frontier results. Involved in three frontier collaborations, active in each.
2. No ML publications — not proposing ML methods, so the committee may see this as outside their scope. Not much to do except frame the different angle as generative.

## Suggestions

**Community service framing.** Frame diffusion not as an afterthought but as service: I'm doing this to increase the AI literacy and frontier capability of French researchers. This resonates with CNRS's mission.

**Benchmarks.** Name them as a deliverable. Include budget line for running and making available to community. Argue cosmology is ideal for realistic scientific benchmarks (taste, multi-criteria judgment, interconnected decisions). We've touched this in the 5pp but Sabouret wants it more concrete.

**Sovereignty tension.** Acknowledge French AI objectives (sovereignty, frugality, interpretability). Commit to using Mistral. But also argue for comparing frontier models against sovereign ones — understanding the gap in science applications matters. Cite METR. Frame: these values AND state of the art, not either/or.
nodes.outcome: Compelling proposal per Sabouret. Key risks: INS2I lab preference (CosmoStat may not qualify), weak ML track record. Key suggestions: community service framing (diffusion as service to French research), propose benchmarks (budget line + cosmology rationale), sovereignty tension (use Mistral + compare frontier models, cite METR gap). Nicolas checking INS2I question with a contact.

nodes.title: Email Choose France: eligibility
nodes.body:
Email the Choose France contact listed on the call. Two questions: (1) Am I eligible as a postdoc <2 years after PhD? Previous Choose France calls reportedly required permanent positions. (2) Is there a preference or requirement for host labs affiliated with INS2I (the CNRS informatics unit)? Context: CosmoStat is CEA/IRFU, not INS2I.

## Comments
**2026-03-16 16:02** — Sent 2026-03-16 from Paris-Saclay account. Final version mentions DAp AIM co-tutelle CEA/CNRS (stronger than just 'CosmoStat, CEA'). Two questions: postdoc eligibility + INS2I/CEA lab preference.
nodes.outcome: Reply from Jalal Fadili (délégué scientifique INS2I, directeur AISSAI) same day. (1) Postdocs eligible — this is a CNRS AI program, not the ministry Choose France stricto sensu. (2) Host lab must have CNRS as tutelle, but need not be INS2I. A lab with exclusively CEA tutelle cannot be a host. DAp/AIM co-tutelle status needs verification.

nodes.title: INS2I lab eligibility
nodes.body:
Sabouret raised concern that the call may prefer or require host labs associated with INS2I (Institut des Sciences de l'Information et de leurs Interactions), the CNRS informatics unit. CosmoStat (CEA) and IAS may not be INS2I labs. If true, need to identify INS2I-affiliated labs with cosmology or AI-for-science fit. Nicolas is also checking with a contact. Two tracks: his inquiry + our email to Choose France.

## Comments
**2026-03-16 22:20** — Resolved by Fadili reply: no INS2I requirement. CNRS tutelle required, not INS2I specifically. CosmoStat as pure CEA may not qualify, but DAp/AIM under CNRS co-tutelle may. IAS (UMR 8617) is fine. Key question now: does AIM's CNRS co-tutelle make CosmoStat eligible, or do we need to name AIM/DAp explicitly?
**2026-03-16 22:45** — Correction: CosmoStat is NOT pure CEA. It sits within AIM (UMR 7158, CNRS/CEA/Université Paris Cité). CNRS is tutelle. Both host labs qualify: CosmoStat via AIM UMR 7158, IAS UMR 8617. No lab eligibility issue.
nodes.outcome: Resolved. No INS2I requirement (Fadili). Both labs have CNRS tutelle: CosmoStat (AIM, UMR 7158) and IAS (UMR 8617). No eligibility issue.

nodes.title: Benchmarks proposal
nodes.body: Sabouret strongly endorsed proposing realistic scientific benchmarks. Include in budget (compute for running + making available to community). Argue cosmology is ideal: multi-criteria judgment, taste, percent-level precision, interconnected decisions. Already touched on in 5pp but should be more concrete — named deliverable, not just motivation. Connect to METR-style evaluations but for science.

nodes.title: Sovereignty-frontier tension
nodes.body: Sabouret's advice: acknowledge alignment with French AI objectives (sovereignty, frugality, interpretability, verification) and commit to using Mistral. But also argue for comparing frontier models against sovereign ones in science applications — understanding the gap matters. Cite METR results showing performance gap (Claude/GPT vs Mistral). Frame: I care about these values AND need to push state of the art for fundamental science. Already partially addressed in 5pp but needs sharper framing.

nodes.title: Sam 5pp review
nodes.outcome: Very well written, central argument strong. Four detail-level notes: (1) make tangible deliverables + timelines clearer, (2) some parts too anecdotal, (3) AI jargon at opening needs rewording for non-specialists (METR sentence), (4) mention HDR co-supervision for PhD students. Converges with Sabouret on benchmarks (#1) and accessibility (#3).

nodes.title: ResearchGym paper
nodes.body:
ResearchGym (2602.15112, Garikaparthi et al.) — benchmark for closed-loop AI research agents. Closest existing work to what we're proposing for cosmology.

**What's relevant:**
- Task construction: skeleton repos with method removed, grading scripts, contamination-aware (post-cutoff papers). This is the template for cosmology benchmark tasks.
- Execution-based grading, not LLM judges — aligns with our emphasis on rigor.
- Found capability-reliability gap: agents occasionally hit SOTA but only 1/15 runs improved over baseline. The gap IS the research question.
- Failure modes (overconfidence, optimization myopia, context degradation) map directly to what we'd expect in cosmology pipelines.
- Single GPU, 24hr budget — accessible by design.

**What's missing (our opportunity):**
- All tasks are ML/NLP — no physical science tasks, no cosmology.
- No multi-criteria judgment or taste — their tasks have single metrics. Cosmology requires balancing systematics budgets, model choices, blinding.
- No long-horizon pipeline work (their longest is 24hr). Survey cosmology analyses take months of iteration.
- No domain knowledge evaluation — do agents understand the physics, or just optimize code?

**Connection to our proposal:** We should cite this as the state of the art and frame cosmology benchmarks as the next frontier — where the analysis complexity, precision requirements, and multi-step decision-making far exceed current ML engineering benchmarks.

nodes.title: Pleias consortium
nodes.body:
Anastasia Stasenko (CEO, Pleias) responded 2026-03-18 to Dec 2025 outreach from GenerationAI. Pleias is partner in EU consortium developing AI use cases, including LLM-based verification of scientific claims. Three-stage pipeline: claim extraction, evidence retrieval, claim verification (support/refute/NEI). Current partners 'not really that advanced' — Pleias wants something innovative, not just fine-tuning. Proposes collaboration + potential paper.

Strategic landscape: three complementary approaches to the same problem.
- Pleias/consortium: fine-tune smaller LLMs for verification pipeline
- Lightcone (François): scientific insights DB + reproducibility + agentic layer (ASTRA schema)
- Cail: agentic frontier models, verification as process infrastructure

Natural experiment: can fine-tuned domain models beat frontier models at verification? Cail's thesis says frontier + agentic > specialized + pipeline.

For CNRS proposal: French AI startup with govt partnerships + EU consortium = strong evidence for verification pillar. Light or deep mention both strengthen application.

Blocked on: conversation with François (2026-03-19) re: whether he wants to be looped in or sees Pleias as Lightcone competitor. Reply to Anastasia after that conversation.

Key idea from Cail: Pleias could adopt ASTRA schema for their claims and literature index — interoperability between Lightcone and Pleias infrastructure.

nodes.title: Talk to Jean-Luc
nodes.body:
Haven't yet discussed Rising Talents application with Jean-Luc Starck (CosmoStat director). Two reasons to do this soon:

1. Jean-Luc is close with Jalal Fadili (délégué scientifique INS2I, directeur AISSAI) — who already replied helpfully to our eligibility email. Jean-Luc likely has tacit knowledge about how the program works, what the committee values, how proposals are read.

2. As a non-French applicant, working the institutional network matters. The CNRS system runs on relationships and informal channels. Jean-Luc is the most natural bridge.

This is separate from the letter of support he'd write as lab director — this is about gathering intelligence on the program itself.
nodes.outcome: Already spoke to Jean-Luc early in the process — he's supportive of the application. Doesn't know about the Fadili connection. Still a channel for program intelligence if needed later.

nodes.title: Fix CosmoStat naming
nodes.body: Must not mention CosmoStat in proposal — the lab is UMR AIM, DAp (Département d'Astrophysique). CosmoStat is a sub-team. Stéphane Mathis and CNRS people dislike it being referenced as if it's the lab. Audit 5pp and fix all references. Once at most, preferably zero.
nodes.outcome: Done. One CosmoStat mention remains (lab integration section, line 222). All others replaced with UMR AIM or removed. Also rewrote UMR AIM description to accurately reflect the lab's breadth (not just cosmology).

nodes.title: Follow up Stéphane Mathis
nodes.body: Emailed 2026-03-07, no reply after 12 days. Need his sign-off as DAp director. Per François, hosts need to actively want you — so this isn't just a formality. Plan: send him the 5pp (not full dossier) to give him less to sort through. Fix naming first.

nodes.title: Email Nicolas: INS2I resolved
nodes.body: Thank Nicolas for raising INS2I question at lunch. Share good news: Fadili confirmed no INS2I requirement, both labs have CNRS tutelle. Keep door warm for potential LISN as second host (don't mention this yet).

nodes.title: Email IAS lab heads
nodes.body: Push harder on IAS as second host lab. Need to email Laura and Adélie.

nodes.title: Second host lab: IAS or LISN
nodes.body: IAP removed from 5pp. Considering IAS (UMR 8617, CNRS) or LISN (Nicolas Sabouret's lab) as second host. IAS has survey cosmology depth (Euclid, SPT connections). LISN has AI/CS strength and Sabouret's endorsement. Haven't decided yet — proposal currently only mentions AIM.

nodes.title: Flagship: UNIONS + Euclid CMBX
nodes.outcome: UNIONS and Euclid CMB cross-correlations are the two flagship deliverable streams. SPT and kinematic lensing are portfolio breadth, not primary. Rationale: UNIONS small team = ideal agentic science demo; Euclid CMBX = tightest constraints on dark energy and neutrino masses. SPT connections remain (MOU, deep field maps) but framed as supporting Euclid work.

nodes.title: 5pp over page limit
nodes.body: After refocus edits, proposal compiles to ~6 pages of content + 1 page refs. Need to cut ~1 page. Lab integration section (ecosystem connections: DATAIA, AISSAI, PostGenAI) is the main candidate for compression. Verification/diffusion section also substantial.

nodes.title: Survey metrics
nodes.outcome: Author counts: DES Y3 3×2pt = 170 (Abbott+ 2022). KiDS-1000 3×2pt = 34 (Heymans+ 2021). KiDS-Legacy shear = 38 (Wright+ 2025). HSC Y3 shear = 38 (Dalal+ 2023). Euclid consortium ~2600 members (overview paper 2957 authors, Mellier+ 2024). UNIONS overview = 89 (Gwyn+ 2025). Euclid DR schedule: Q2 June 2026, DR1 Oct 2026, DR2 March 2029, DR3 Oct 2031. Moriond Cosmology 2026: March 22-29, La Thuile.

nodes.title: Application snapshot March 2026
nodes.body:
Built 2026-03-22 from Overleaf commit b09cde4.

**5pp research programme** (main.pdf → cnrs-5pp-research-programme.pdf): 7 pages (5 content + 2 bibliography). Four sections: (1) Bitter lesson extended to science, METR scaling, domain examples; (2) Agentic cosmology program — CMB×Euclid, UNIONS, deep fields, kinematic lensing; (3) Mentorship, diffusion, young researchers; (4) Verification as epistemic sovereignty, ClusterAI alignment. EB Garamond/Raleway typography.

**Full dossier** (dossier.tex → cnrs-dossier.pdf): 16 pages. Five parts: CV (career + 37 publications), research report (CMB lensing, UNIONS, kinematic lensing, AU Mic), publications narrative (37 papers, 3 highlighted), research programme (5pp), referee contacts. Unified document with consistent styling and section dividers.

Both PDFs at project root: cnrs-5pp-research-programme.pdf, cnrs-dossier.pdf.
nodes.outcome: Application documents compiled and ready for review. The 5pp is the intellectual core; the dossier is the submission package. Remaining before April 6: referee contacts (3), lab director sign-off (2), publication PDFs (3).
